Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Understanding the Go Runtime: The Scheduler 71 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. Vite 8.0 Is Out 63 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. WolfIP: Lightweight TCP/IP stack with no dynamic memory allocations 114 upvotes, 14 comments
  4. The Met releases high-def 3D scans of 140 famous art objects 255 upvotes, 50 comments
  5. IMG_0416 (2024) 38 upvotes, 4 comments
  6. Bubble Sorted Amen Break 297 upvotes, 91 comments
  7. Malus – Clean Room as a Service 1149 upvotes, 424 comments
  8. Show HN: OneCLI – Vault for AI Agents in Rust 137 upvotes, 40 comments
  9. Shall I implement it? No 1090 upvotes, 417 comments
  10. Forcing Flash Attention onto a TPU and Learning the Hard Way 54 upvotes, 13 comments
  11. Long overlooked as crucial to life, fungi start to get their due 116 upvotes, 38 comments
  12. Reversing memory loss via gut-brain communication 277 upvotes, 112 comments
  13. Willingness to look stupid 106 upvotes, 37 comments
  14. Celebrating Interesting Flickr Technologies 14 upvotes, 2 comments
  15. Document poisoning in RAG systems: How attackers corrupt AI's sources 98 upvotes, 39 comments
  16. Innocent woman jailed after being misidentified using AI facial recognition 524 upvotes, 279 comments
  17. Launch HN: IonRouter (YC W26) – High-throughput, low-cost inference 60 upvotes, 24 comments
  18. “This is not the computer for you” 214 upvotes, 113 comments
  19. Show HN: Axe – A 12MB binary that replaces your AI framework 169 upvotes, 100 comments
  20. Hyperlinks in Terminal Emulators 17 upvotes, 5 comments
  21. Bringing Chrome to ARM64 Linux Devices 81 upvotes, 44 comments
  22. Big data on the cheapest MacBook 333 upvotes, 266 comments
  23. The Unpredicted vs. the Over-Expected 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. Are LLM merge rates not getting better? 131 upvotes, 120 comments
  25. How people woke up before alarm clocks 38 upvotes, 29 comments
  26. ATMs didn’t kill bank teller jobs, but the iPhone did 378 upvotes, 409 comments
  27. US private credit defaults hit record 9.2% in 2025, Fitch says 320 upvotes, 393 comments
  28. Why Technology Makes Us More Productive but Not Richer 4 upvotes, 1 comments
  29. Grief and the AI split 101 upvotes, 163 comments
  30. Can you instruct a robot to make a PBJ sandwich? 19 upvotes, 25 comments

Last generated 2:00pm, 13 March 2026. Source here.
